meaning: adv.使麻木地,使失去感觉地
examples: The street was lined with numbingly ordinary aluminum-sided split-levels, circa 1962.
What could be better on a numbingly cold winter night than a hot pot to warm your heart?
In 1997-98, Washington numbingly junked up the tax code with additional incomprehensible provisions.
The tax code is mind-numbingly complex, as dedicated to manipulating individual behavior as to raising government funds.
